message:
feat(membrane): add Clifford algebra, microlocal analysis, and Serre spectral sequence controllers

Introduce three new runtime math controllers and wire them into the
kernel snapshot and routing infrastructure:

- clifford.rs (new): Clifford algebra controller for geometric alignment
  monitoring — tracks alignment observations, regime classification
  (stable/drifting/chaotic), and provides algebraic invariant signals
  for the routing decision pipeline
- microlocal.rs (new): microlocal analysis controller implementing
  wavefront set tracking across stratified fault boundaries — monitors
  active strata, propagation failure rates (EWMA), and fault boundary
  detection for singularity-aware routing decisions
- serre_spectral.rs (new): Serre spectral sequence controller modeling
  differential density and non-trivial cell tracking across filtration
  layers — provides lifting failure counts and invariant class detection
  for algebraic topology-informed stage ordering

Integration changes:
- mod.rs: register all three new modules, import their types, add
  snapshot fields (microlocal_active_strata, serre_max_differential,
  clifford_alignment_regime, etc.) to RuntimeKernelSnapshot
- fusion.rs: minor adjustment to kernel fusion controller

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
